channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iMessage still lags behind its peers. 4 ways Apple should update it for iOS 19
item.description: Few apps are as inextricably linked to the iPhone as Apple’s Messages. Introduced with the original iPhone almost 18 years ago, the app (then called “Text”) has become the primary messenger for most iPhone users worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk sells X to his own xAI for $33 billion in all-stock deal
item.description: Elon Musk has sold social media site X to his own xAI artificial intelligence company in a $33 billion all-stock deal, the billionaire announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk says he sold X to his AI company
item.description: Elon Musk on Friday evening announced he has sold his social media company, X, to xAI, his artificial intelligence company, in a deal that values X at $33 billion.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X. "xAI and X’s futures are intertwined. Today, we officially take the step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent," Musk wrote in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ubuntu namespace vulnerability should be addressed quickly: Expert
item.description: Linux admins who have enabled the unprivileged user namespace restriction in their recent Ubuntu environments should take action to close three new vulnerabilities that allow a threat actor to bypass the supposed protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A Nvidia chip shortage is coming, warns major Chinese server maker
item.description: One of China’s largest server makers, H3C, has flagged potential shortages of Nvidia’s H20 chip, the most advanced AI processor legally available domestically under U.S. export controls, in a client notice seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to know about earthquakes like the one that hit Myanmar
item.description: A major 7.7 magnitude earthquake that originated near Mandalay, Myanmar shook the Earth as far as Bangkok.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Modernize enterprise PC fleets to ensure AI ambitions deliver business success
item.description: Investing in the right kind of PCs helps businesses innovate, become more secure and adapt to new ways of working. They also ensure that enterprises have a foundation from which to implement crucial AI-driven improvements that can boost productivity and growth.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Dating is even more partisan in the Trump era
item.description: Donald Trump’s return to office has bolstered a wave of apps. Bluesky spiked in November; Signal is seeing renewed attention. Niche dating apps are also raking in new users—both from the right and left. Americans are dating across party lines less and less.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Marvel sleuths think they've solved the case about Avengers: Doomsday's story, and it's all down to a single chair's shadow
item.description: Avengers 5's cast reveal livestream has spawned two new big Marvel fan theories about its plot and a popular character's return.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Tiny Silicon Valley drugmaker soars on FDA win to treat never-ending hunger
item.description: Shares of the Redwood City-based Soleno Therapeutics climbed 38% — the biggest one-day gain since September 2023 — to their highest level in more than eight years.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The RACI matrix: Your blueprint for project success
item.description: Having managed and rescued dozens of projects, and helped others do so, I’ve noted that there is always one critical success factor (CSF) that has either been effectively addressed or missed/messed up: clarity around the roles and responsibilities for each project participant and key stakeholder.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nueve tendencias de CRM para 2025: la IA redefine la ecuación del cliente
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) tiene el potencial de transformar todos los aspectos de una empresa, pero sobre todo la gestión de las relaciones con los clientes (CRM), las funciones comerciales de cara al exterior de ventas, marketing y servicio al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk privately pressured Reddit CEO Steve Huffman on content moderation: report
item.description: X owner Elon Musk was privately messaging with Reddit CEO Steve Huffman while also putting public pressure on the social media company’s content moderation efforts, The Verge reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The slim phone era is (finally) back
item.description: Smartphones have been around long enough that, to the casual observer, their designs seem to have hit a plateau. And on a functional level, that’s more or less true—we’re all essentially holding the same six-inch-ish rectangle, aside from the occasional foldable exception.
item.dc:creator: Sam Byford, Fast Company

item.title: Researchers claim their protocol can create truly random numbers on a current quantum computer
item.description: A team that included researchers at a US bank says it has created a protocol that can generate certified truly random numbers, opening the possibility that current generation quantum computers can be used for secure applications in finance, cryptography, cybersecurity, and privacy.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: State Department announces approval of armed drones sale to Qatar for nearly $2 billion
item.description: The United States has given an initial approval for the energy-rich Mideast nation of Qatar to buy eight armed MQ-9B Predator drones for its military, an estimated purchase worth nearly $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Adobe announces AI agents for customer interaction
item.description: Adobe has announced Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, an intelligent reasoning engine that allows AI agents to perform complex decision-making and problem-solving tasks to accelerate customer experience orchestration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From automation to transformation: How AI is reshaping business
item.description: Are you using artificial intelligence (AI) to do the same things you’ve always done, just more efficiently? If so, you’re only scratching the surface.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fired FTC commissioners sue Trump
item.description: Two Federal Trade Commission (FTC) members fired by President Trump sued him Thursday, setting up another major test of his administration’s expansionist view of presidential authority over independent agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge allows newspaper copyright lawsuit against OpenAI to proceed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has ruled that The New York Times and other newspapers can proceed with a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft seeking to end the practice of using their stories to train artificial intelligence chatbots. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump floats reducing tariffs on China to secure TikTok deal
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday floated reducing tariffs on China to get a TikTok divestiture deal done as the April 5 deadline nears.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE staffer allegedly ran company providing services to hacking group
item.description: US Department of Government Efficiency tech advisor Edward Coristine previously ran a small infrastructure provider that offered services to a cybercriminal group, it has been alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Signalgate: There’s an IT lesson here
item.description: You know how IT admins are always warning employees about best practices for security? They’re always mandating which apps to use, which to avoid and which devices can safely connect to corporate networks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: No application can eliminate human error: Signal’s head defends the app
item.description: When the editor-in-chief of The Atlantic, Jeff Goldberg, was accidentally added to a Signal conversation, things took a surprising turn.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
